title: Recovery Respite Facility Operations

fields.vision: Recovery Respite Facility Operations is presented as a historical or operational case-study room.
fields.mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from case definition to actors, constraints, causal mechanism, analogy limits, operational decision point, and limited lesson. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.
fields.built_components: Visible public components: 53 kept Evidence Sections, native navigation, Review Notes, source-use plan, APA lineage, and source lineage claim limit. The public package remains text-focused and does not claim unverified runtime state.
fields.source_utilization: George and Bennett. Loads case-study design, process tracing, and theory-development discipline. Allison and Zelikow. Loads decision-model plurality: rational actor, organizational process, and bureaucratic politics. Jervis. Loads perception, misperception, signaling, and adversary interpretation risks. Schelling. Loads coercion, bargaining, deterrence, and strategic commitment logic. No page-specific page source plan row was matched. The source section is therefore a method and source background and source-use plan, not a claim that every sentence has passage-level review.
fields.plan: Plan: name the governing case, distinguish evidence from analogy, state the transfer rule, add counter-analogies, and document which operational or historical claims remain provisional. The project source notes below should be converted into authored subsections only after the page-specific claim limit and source-use decisions are settled.
fields.status: Status: native public port complete for upload and navigation; substantive source-project verification remains limited by the source lineage ceiling and the original project governance records. The page separates public presentation from project completion; completion depends on the status and remaining-gap fields.
fields.remaining_gaps: Remaining gaps: several pages still need cleaner public explanation of why each case was chosen and what exact decision problem it is meant to illuminate. The kept Evidence Sections below preserve project substance for close reading; final public prose should be authored from those signals rather than copied mechanically.
fields.detailed_source_vision: Recovery Respite Facility Operations is presented as a historical or operational case-study room. Its public purpose is to show how historical cases, simulations, or operational constraints can discipline present judgment without collapsing into simplistic analogy. Source lead: Post-acute withdrawal managed. No seizure history. Mild sleep disturbance and anxiety. Medically stable for respite-level care.
fields.detailed_source_mechanism: The working mechanism is to move from case definition to actors, constraints, causal mechanism, analogy limits, operational decision point, and limited lesson. The reader should be able to ask what enters the room, what transformation occurs, what judgment or work product leaves, and which gate prevents overclaiming.

source_derived_signals.text: You are a respite staff member facing a real-time crisis. Each scenario presents a multi-stage situation where your choices have consequences. Every response is scored on three dimensions: client safety, therapeutic alliance, and regulatory compliance. There are no do-overs. Choose carefully.

Each support plan is built from the ASAM assessment, addressing the highest-acuity dimensions first. Plans follow harm reduction principles: meet people where they are, prioritize safety over abstinence demands, and build on existing strengths. A good plan is the client's plan — not ours.

These scenarios are based on real clinical situations. The "right" answers are drawn from evidence-based frameworks (SAMHSA, C-SSRS, Motivational Interviewing). Your composite score determines your clinical readiness grade.
High motivation — immediately self-referred. Relapse occurred within existing recovery framework (AA, Oxford House). Needs to process what happened without abandoning what worked.

maintenance_rule: Update this blueprint before strengthening public claims on the website. Do not use word count, module count, or status percentage as a substitute for concrete project substance.
claim_ceiling_rule: Public claims may rise only when the built components, source use, status, and remaining gaps are updated here and reflected in the rendered page.

public_standard_check.Mechanism: It connects source material, project components, implementation state, and next actions so the work can be inspected rather than merely asserted.
public_standard_check.What is built: The supporting evidence is the named page, blueprint, packet, project file, source record, or implementation page described in the document.
public_standard_check.Source use: Sources are used as method and source background, domain grounding, or project history; direct quotation and stronger factual claims require passage-level review.
public_standard_check.Plan: Bind claims to exact source anchors, visible implementation evidence, or a narrower public claim.
public_standard_check.Status: Current support record for the professional package unless explicitly marked archival.
public_standard_check.Remaining Gaps: Passage-level source check, implementation verification, citation cleanup, and proportional public wording remain open where evidence is incomplete.
